senses.definition: to bargain again, especially to negotiate a new or revised price or terms
examples: They decided to rebargain after realizing the initial offer was too high.
The seller agreed to rebargain once the buyer pointed out additional flaws.

senses.definition: short for reinforcing bars; steel bars embedded in concrete to increase its strength
examples: The foundation used several rebars to reinforce the concrete.
They inspected the rebars for any signs of corrosion.

senses.definition: to change the base data or reference point of something, often to recalibrate calculations or records
examples: The company decided to rebase its pricing model on inflation-driven costs.
We need to rebase our project timeline after the new constraints.

senses.definition: having had the base data or reference point recalibrated
examples: The projected costs were rebased to reflect current market conditions.
A rebased schedule showed earlier completion dates.

senses.definition: third person singular of rebase; changes the base data or reference point
examples: The team rebases the model every quarter.
They rebases the cost estimates to reflect new inputs.

senses.definition: the act of changing the base data or reference point
examples: Rebasing is necessary when currency values shift dramatically.
The rebasing process improved forecast accuracy.

senses.definition: the new base or reference point after rebase
examples: The rebasis was set to reflect updated market conditions.
Choosing a stable rebasis helped stabilize projections.
